assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); per-eval rates: eval-1 100%, eval-2 100%, eval-3 85% (11/13), eval-4 100%, eval-5 100%; overall pass rate 91%; 2 regression failures in eval-3 triggered sub-task creation |'. Also line 132-133: '#### Eval Quality -- WARN' confirming the Eval Quality component is WARN and drives the combined Test Quality verdict to WARN.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-34: 'An eval result review was detected on this PR: Review ID: 40001, Author: github-actions[bot], Detection criteria met: (1) author is github-actions[bot], (2) body contains ## Eval Results marker, (3) body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer, Result: Eval results extracted and processed for Test Quality assessment'. Lines 37-44 show extracted eval metrics table with per-eval pass rates. Line 71: '40001 (review body) | github-actions[bot] | Eval result review (excluded) | Processed as eval data, not classified as review feedback'.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 6: 'Type: Inline review comment (NOT an eval result review)', line 8: 'Author: reviewer-b (human reviewer)', line 12: 'Classification: Code Change Request'. Lines 16-17 explicitly state: 'It is NOT an eval result review -- it does not meet the three-criteria eval detection test (author is not github-actions[bot], body does not contain ## Eval Results marker, body does not contain sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer)'. Classification reasoning discusses directive language and reviewer intent, with no reference to eval metrics or eval detection.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); per-eval rates: eval-1 100%, eval-2 100%, eval-3 85% (11/13), eval-4 100%, eval-5 100%; overall pass rate 91%'. Lines 48-56 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path'.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 7: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comments classified as suggestions.' Lines 22-28 include both failing assertion texts and evidence. Report line 62: '| Eval-3 failure fix | eval-failure | Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation |'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 20-28: '## Review Context' section contains: Assertion 1 Text: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...', Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2 Text: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...', Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: Both sub-task files include a Target PR section but with a different URL. subtask-1.md line 31: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 19: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. The assertion requires URL 'https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747' (owner: RHEcosystemAppEng) but the actual URL uses owner 'mrizzi'. The GitHub organization/owner does not match.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-tasks investigated; convention upgrade pipeline gap identified as implement-task skill gap |'. Lines 75-83 contain the full Root-Cause Investigation section with Verdict: DONE, universality test, phase attribution (implement-task phase), and classification (Skill gap). The verdict is DONE, not N/A.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 output files are Markdown reporting/description files: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) appear in the outputs directory. The outputs consist exclusively of verification artifacts (reports, classifications, sub-task descriptions, and criterion evaluations).

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 89-91: Scope Containment PASS based on 'PR files match task specification exactly. Both style-conventions.md and SKILL.md are listed in the task's Files to Modify section and are the only files changed' -- no eval references. Lines 99-101: Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS based on 'No sensitive patterns detected. The PR adds only documentation and instruction text in Markdown files' -- independent of eval data. Lines 108-118: Acceptance Criteria PASS with 7/7 criteria each evaluated against specific PR diff content (step 6a, 6b, 6c functionality). criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each analyze PR diff content independently. None of these checks reference eval results in their reasoning.
